review_summary: Textual grounding is sufficient for concept-focused questions, and no separate video clip is needed.

question_bank.question: Which deployment practice best addresses the ethical risks of NLG systems?
choices: Test for harmful, biased, toxic, and adversarially triggered outputs before deployment.
Assume fluent outputs are always safe and truthful.
Remove all safeguards so users can test every possible prompt.
Evaluate only whether the model can produce long text.
question_bank.explanation: Safety requires checking more than fluency. A deployed generator should be assessed for harmful content, bias, toxicity, jailbreak vulnerability, and robustness to adversarial prompts.

question_bank.question: Why is truthfulness a separate concern from fluency in large language models?
choices: A model can produce persuasive, well-written text that is factually wrong.
A fluent model cannot contain stereotypes from its data.
Truthfulness is guaranteed by sampling-based decoding.
Factual errors occur only when a model refuses a prompt.
question_bank.explanation: Fluent wording can make incorrect information sound convincing. Reliability therefore requires explicit factuality checks rather than judging only whether the text reads naturally.
